g.text: Memory Access Patterns: GPU Coalescing Impact
Note: Stride > 1 causes severe divergence in memory controller.

div.p: Memory Coalescing allows the GPU to merge multiple memory requests from a single warp into a single transaction.
If threads in a warp access a contiguous 128-byte block, the hardware performs one fetch. If they are strided, the hardware performs 32 separate fetches, wasting 97% of the fetched data.
